parameters.name: user_app_id.user_id
parameters.description: Note user_id 'me' is reserved - it is the alias for the id of authorized user

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
(optional URL parameter) The page number. Pagination is used to split the results into chunks.
Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description:
(optional URL parameter) The number of results that will be contained in each page. Defaults
to 128.

parameters.name: additional_fields
parameters.description: (optional URL parameter) List of additional fields to be included in the response. Currently supported: all, stars, outputs, presets, counts

parameters.name: show_replicas
parameters.description: If true, show replica counts for models.

parameters.name: sort_ascending
parameters.description:
Sorting options:
Whether to sort in ascending order. If false, will order in descending order.

parameters.name: sort_by_name
parameters.description: Whether to order by the name

parameters.name: sort_by_num_inputs
parameters.description: Whether to order by the number of training inputs

parameters.name: sort_by_modified_at
parameters.description:
Whether to order by the modified_at time of the latest model version.
If none of the sort options is set to true, will sort by modified_at.

parameters.name: sort_by_created_at
parameters.description: Whether to order by the created_at

parameters.name: sort_by_star_count
parameters.description: Whether to order by count of stars

parameters.name: sort_by_relevance
parameters.description: Whether to order by search query relevance. Can only be used if search is not empty.

parameters.name: model_type_id
parameters.description:
Filtering options:
Filter models by the specific model_type_id. See ListModelTypes for the list of ModelType.Id's
supported.

parameters.name: trained_only
parameters.description: If true, we only return models that have the status MODEL_TRAINED, which includes non-trainable model types.

parameters.name: input_fields
parameters.description:
The list of input fields to the model.
For example, you can specify 'image', which will return models that make inferences on images like visual-classifier models.

parameters.name: output_fields
parameters.description:
The list of output fields to the model.
For example, you can specify 'regions[...].data.concepts', which will return visual-detector models.

parameters.name: license
parameters.description: Filter by the license of the model version

parameters.name: featured_only
parameters.description: If true, we only return models that are handpicked by clarifai staff

parameters.name: starred_only
parameters.description: If true, we only return models that are starred by the requesting user

parameters.name: toolkits
parameters.description: List of toolkit tags to filter by

parameters.name: use_cases
parameters.description: List of use_case tags to filter by

parameters.name: languages
parameters.description: List of language tags to filter by

parameters.name: dont_fetch_from_main
bookmark
parameters.description:
Filter models by bookmark. If set, only return bookmarked models. Otherwise none bookmarked models only.
Note: you can not filter `trained_only` and bookmark at the same time.
When filter by bookmark, we will return trained and untrained models.

parameters.name: model_version_ids
parameters.description: Filter by the model version ids. If set, only return the model of these versions.

parameters.name: license_type
parameters.description: Filter by LicenseType

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter by Source

parameters.name: creator
parameters.description: Filter by Creator

parameters.name: min_replicas
parameters.description: Filter by model versions runners with replicas >= min_replicas.

parameters.name: visibility.gettable
parameters.description:
- UNKNOWN_VISIBILITY: Default value not allowed.
 - PRIVATE: PRIVATE requires collaborator or team permissions in order to GET this resource.
 - ORG: ORG requires you to be in the same org in order to GET this resource, but don't have to be a
teammate or collaborator.
 - PUBLIC: PUBLIC opens up GET access to the resource to any user on the platform even if they are not
a teammate or collaborator.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description:
Searching options:
Specify a search parameter in order to perform keyword search on the
following fields of the model:
  - id
  - name
  - description
  - notes
  - user_id (unless user_app_id.user_id is already set)

Keywords are both normalized for search (so searching for "satisfy" matches "satisfied")
and used for partial prefix-matching (so searching for "clari" matches "clarifai").

NOTE: Both the list of fields searched and the exact keyword matching
rules are subject to change and not guaranteed to be backwards-compatible.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description:
Query name, description and id fields, that can contain the words in the query string. Does NOT support wildcards - full words only. Supports operators "OR" and "-" as NOT.
Deprecated: use search instead.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Filter by the description and id of the model. This supports wildcard queries like "gen*" to match "general" as an example.
Deprecated: use search instead.

parameters.name: filter_by_user_id
parameters.description:
Extends the name filter to include the user_id of the application owner that the model belongs to.
Deprecated: use search instead of name.
